Name: I Can't Get Wit That
Notes: Unreleased music video from 1994 for the song "I Can't Get Wit That" which was released as a Side B to the single "In My Lifetime" from 1995.

Name:
Can't Knock the Hustle
(feat. Veronica) (prod. Knobody, Nomad & Sean C)
Notes: Confirmed in an interview with the song's producer, Knobody, who said that a space was left for the hook, which the Latin singer Veronica was originally supposed to sing, but then Mary J. Blige stepped in after Roc-A-Fella had contacted her and she had heard the track and immediately wanted to get involved.

Name:
Bring It On
(feat. Nas & AZ)
Notes: According to Dame Dash, "Nas and AZ was supposed to be on 'Bring It On,' they kept not showing up. That's when we wanted to put out The Firm. They didn't show up. We was meeting and they was saying, 'Yeah,' but they wasn't showing up. We would be waiting and we would be getting offended. So we brought Sauce [Money] and [Big] Jaz on the song"

Name:
Can I Live
(feat. Nas)
Notes: Irv Gotti revealed Nas was supposed to appear on Can I Live
Copy Length:
on a Drink Champs Interview.

"We was waiting for Nas to get on 'Can I Live,'" Gotti revealed at the 2:06:30-minute mark, much to N.O.R.E. and DJ EFN's surprise. "N-gga, no one knows [this] — yeah, they know now. We was waiting for Nas to do the second verse! ... He didn't show. That's the end of the story!"

Name:
Big L - Platinum Plus
(feat. Jay-Z & Big Daddy Kane) (prod. DJ Premier)
Notes: According to DJ Premier on a Interview with Complex, he said that "Jay-Z was supposed to be on ['Platinum Plus'] too. It was supposed to be the three of them, but he never had the chance to do it in the time frame. We really had to turn it in to make the date. So we just went ahead and did it without him"

Name: Do It Again (Put Ya Hands Up)
Anything
Notes: Shorter (and better) version found on Beanie Segul's album "The Truth"

Name: I Just Wanna Love U (Give It 2 Me)

Jay-Z - We Gonna Make It
(prod. The Alchemist)
Notes: When the Alchemist finished producing the instrumental of the song, he first played it for DJ Premier, who liked it. Rapper Nas was originally intended to record a song with the instrumental, but he did not. Jay-Z also heard the beat, but passed as well. Jadakiss had expressed interest in rapping to the beat, and recorded "We Gonna Make It".

Name:
DMX - Heart Of The City (Ain't No Love)
(prod. Kanye West)
Notes: Kanye mentions in "Last Call" that he wanted to give the "Heart Of The City" beat to DMX.

Name:
Ghostface Killah - Girls, Girls, Girls
(prod. Just Blaze)
Notes: Just Blaze confirmed in an interview with The Alchemist that he originally produced the song "Girls, Girls, Girls" for Ghostface Killah. It's unknown if he recorded.

Name:
Prodigy - U Don't Know
(prod. Just Blaze)
Notes: In an interview, Just Blaze said that U Don't Know was originally meant for Prodigy & Busta Rhymes

Name:

(prod. Just Blaze)
Notes: In an interview, Just Blaze said that U Don't Know was originally meant for Prodigy & Busta Rhymes

Name: Fade To Black
Notes: Documentary

Name: Last Call
Notes: Evidence said that he originally made the "Last Call" beat for JAY-Z to use for The Black Album. Evidence showed the beat to Kanye, so that he could give it to JAY-Z, but Kanye instead wanted the beat for himself stating "I'm the new Mr. Rocafella." Kanye eventually did show the beat to JAY-Z as depicted in the Fade To Black documentary, but kept it for himself.

Name: Kanye West - Lucifer Freestyle
Notes: Kanye freestyling over the "Lucifer" beat while showing JAY-Z the beat for the first time, contains the line "I'm from the capital where we murder for capital" which was reused in the offical song. Freestyle showcased in the JAY-Z Fade To Black documentary.

Name:
Kanye West - Barry Bonds
(feat. Lil Wayne & JAY-Z)
Notes: JAY-Z was going to be on "Barry Bonds", "but it was late in the day, so [he] didn't get to do it".

Name:
Jay-Z - On Top Of The World
(prod. J Cole)
Notes: Song that was made to show to JAY-Z in hopes that he'd include it into American Gangsta, but he declined.

Name:

(feat. Rihanna & Kanye West) (prod. Kanye West & No I.D.)
Notes: High-quality rip of the "Run This Town" music video.

Name:

(feat. Kanye West & JAY-Z)
Notes: JAY-Z performing a version of "American Boy" live. No evidence of it ever being studio recorded.

Name:
Dark Fantasy
(prod. No I.D.)
Notes: No I.D. stated that Kanye West's "Dark Fantasy" beat was offered to JAY-Z for The Blueprint 3 before being given to Kanye.

Name: Heartless
Notes: Recorded as a reference track for JAY-Z by Kanye. Same version as the mumble demo. JAY-Z never recorded for it.

Name: So Appalled
Notes: Was originally given to JAY-Z for The Blueprint 3 but he didn't want to use it. It wasn't until he heard CyHi's verse that he decided he wanted to be on the song.

Name: Welcome To Heartbreak
Notes: An alt Cudi hook was recorded in intentions of being on "Already Home". Kanye made Cudi rewrite the hook for his own song. JAY-Z never recorded.

Name:
All The Time
(feat. Mr Hudson) (prod. NO ID)
Notes: In an MTV Article, Kanye apperantly told Mr Hudson that he wanted him to to do the chorus for a track called 'She's Like That All the Time.'. It's is assumed this is a Earlier Version of All The Time due to the chorus on that track being "She Like That All The Time" and it being a Kanye and Jay-Z Track. It's unknown if Mr Hudson ever recorded on the chorus

Name:
T.I. & JAY-Z - Swagga Like Us (Remix)
(feat. Jeezy) (prod. Kanye West & Mike Caren)
Notes: On his blog, Kanye said "I already know what you [gonna] say ... we need Jeezy also ... I know ... I'm sure that's gonna be the official remix."

Name:
Lift Off [Music Video]
(feat. Beyoncé, Don Jazzy, Seal, Mr Hudson, Bankulli & Ricardo Lewis)
Notes: According to a journalists who attended a "private listening session" at the Mercer, "Lift Off" was going to have a music video. It's unknown whether or not this was recorded.

Notes: Lossless music video for "Niggas In Paris".

Name: Watch The Throne (Documentary)
Notes: 10 minute long documentary showing the Watch The Throne sessions. Contains snippets of songs such as "Lift Off" and "Why I Love You".

Name: Live at Coachella 2011 [Video]
Notes: Performance from Coachella 2011, when Kanye headlined. Features very different versions of some songs.

Name: Otis [Live at the 2011 VMA's]
Notes: Kanye and JAY-Z performed "Otis" live at the 2011 VMA's. There are two versions included, one censored and one uncensored.

Name: Victoria's Secret Fashion Show 2011
Notes: Kanye performed at the 2011 Victoria's Secret fashion show, performing "Stronger" and a censored version of the song "Niggas in Paris" alongside JAY-Z called "Legends In Paris".
